title: Accessibility audit + gate (ACCESSIBILITY_RULES.md / P0.13)
business_reason: ACCESSIBILITY_RULES.md required label-associated inputs, aria-labels on icon buttons, visible focus, status by color+text, focus-trapped modals. Audit + fix the gaps and add a gate so it can't regress.

design.fixes: Field now uses explicit htmlFor+id (clones the child to add an id); ModuleTable create inputs get id={f.key}; all standalone selects/inputs (ModuleTable toolbar search/filter/sort/import/row-status, materials-upload, exports, settings-changes) get aria-labels; legacy --finverse-* colors in ModuleTable replaced with Versa tokens.
design.already_compliant: StatusBadge/RiskBadge carry text + a dot (color is never the only indicator); Modal/Drawer trap focus + Escape; LoadingState has aria-busy + sr-only; visible :focus outlines in design.css; semantic <th> headers.
design.gate: check_a11y.py — fails on icon-only buttons without aria-label, without alt, and inputs/selects/textareas without id/aria-label that aren't wrapped in a Field/label. 0 issues. Wired into BUILD_PROCESS step 9.

param_14.question: Are the screens accessible?
param_14.answer: Yes, to the statically-checkable rules: every input is label-associated (Field uses htmlFor+id; standalone controls have aria-labels), icon-only buttons have aria-labels, status is shown by color AND text + a dot, modals/drawers trap focus and close on Escape, loading has aria-busy + screen-reader text, focus rings are visible, and tables use semantic headers. check_a11y.py enforces 0 issues.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: full audit beyond static checks (contrast ratios, keyboard-trap testing, screen-reader pass, reduced-motion).
